jokes.text: You go to jail if a cop doesn't like you. They can't send you to prison without knowing you're poor.
jokes.setup: Character explains the difference between jail and prison after being asked
jokes.punchline: Cynical take that prison requires proving you're poor while jail is just cop bias
jokes.explanation: Sharp, cynical observation about the justice system with economic punch. Well-structured parallel that rings true.

jokes.text: By which I mean figure out what makes a DJ good, or bad, or different from a phone or laptop.
jokes.setup: Character says they're looking for a good DJ
jokes.punchline: Admits they don't understand what makes DJs different from basic technology
jokes.explanation: Relatable modern observation about technology making traditional roles obsolete.

jokes.text: When you asked if I knew PowerPoint, I said, yes. What did I really mean?
jokes.setup: Chang begins what should be a PowerPoint presentation
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he lied about knowing PowerPoint
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang incompetence, perfectly in character. The meta-awareness of his own deception adds layers.

jokes.text: But I'm no magician. I'm just a guy that knows PowerPoint.
jokes.setup: Chang's magic trick with the egg fails
jokes.punchline: Claims to 'just' know PowerPoint after demonstrating complete incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic callback to his earlier lie. The deadpan confidence while failing spectacularly is peak Chang.

jokes.text: Why did they need marketing, they're amazing.
jokes.setup: Dean mentions guerrilla marketing
jokes.punchline: Character thinks he means actual gorillas, which would be amazing
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay joke but executed efficiently. Not groundbreaking but functional.

jokes.text: It sounds like brain Windexing.
jokes.setup: Discussion of covert advertising affecting the brain
jokes.punchline: Compares mental manipulation to cleaning glass with brand name product
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that's both specific and absurd. 'Brain Windexing' is a perfectly coined phrase.

jokes.text: School is cancelled. The Honda Fit is happening. It's finally happening.
jokes.setup: Dean learns about Honda Fit from apparent guerrilla marketer
jokes.punchline: Immediately cancels school in manic excitement over a car
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean behavior - abandoning responsibilities for consumer excitement. The repetition builds manic energy.

jokes.text: I'm just going to give this school's assets a quick freezy wheezy.
jokes.setup: Dean needs to freeze school assets to buy Honda
jokes.punchline: Uses childish 'freezy wheezy' term for serious financial action
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean infantile language for adult concepts. The casual financial irresponsibility is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I think so, but sometimes we just hang out and say funny stuff.
jokes.setup: Question about whether meeting is over
jokes.punchline: Meta acknowledgment that they just hang around being funny
jokes.explanation: Self-aware joke about the nature of sitcom ensemble hanging out. Meta without being obnoxious.

jokes.text: I mean, is he black Pierce, old Troy, or Shirley without a giant purse?
jokes.setup: Discussion of where new character Elroy fits in group dynamic
jokes.punchline: Reduces former characters to reductive stereotypes while trying to categorize replacement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on how sitcoms replace characters with similar archetypes. The 'giant purse' detail is perfect specificity.

jokes.text: Did he own a rainbow? Was he the group's pharmacist?
jokes.setup: Elroy asks what was special about Troy after group won't explain
jokes.punchline: Suggests increasingly absurd explanations for their reverence
jokes.explanation: Elroy's dry delivery makes the absurd suggestions funnier. Good escalation from rainbow ownership to drug dealing.

jokes.text: Troy was very gifted at steel drums.
jokes.setup: Elroy demands information about what made Troy special
jokes.punchline: Abed provides completely mundane, specific skill as explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax after the group's reverent buildup. Steel drums is so specific and random it's brilliant.

jokes.text: That won't pay off immediately, but it's gonna pay off. And that Jeff, is the truth.
jokes.setup: Abed tells obvious lie about Troy's steel drum skills
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary about comedy payoffs while claiming to tell truth
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed meta-awareness about comedy writing while participating in the bit. Layers of self-reference.

jokes.text: That's how it works?
jokes.setup: Someone asks Chang if he lost the egg with dollar bill
jokes.punchline: Chang genuinely confused about how his own magic trick was supposed to work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang moment - attempting something without understanding it. The genuine confusion sells it.

jokes.text: Sub. Don't say it. Don't ever say that name without compensation.
jokes.setup: Annie mentions Britta's ex who worked for sandwich franchise
jokes.punchline: Britta's trauma-level response to sandwich brand name, demanding payment
jokes.explanation: Good setup for episode's guerrilla marketing theme. Britta's specific trauma about corporate names is funny and character-driven.

jokes.text: The hub of a quality camping experience is a safe and reliable generator.
jokes.setup: Character appears to be having normal conversation
jokes.punchline: Launches into obvious product pitch mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Sets up guerrilla marketing theme. The sudden shift to sales pitch language is obvious but functional.

jokes.text: Sub. No, no, no, no, that's my sandwich name. I'm my own man now. I'm a free, reliable, quality man, named Rick.
jokes.setup: Rick introduces himself with his old sandwich-associated nickname
jokes.punchline: Declares independence but uses Honda advertising language to describe himself
jokes.explanation: Clever way to show he's switched from one corporate identity to another. 'Reliable, quality' are car ad words.

jokes.text: Can you actually crouch down a little bit cuz you're taller than my instructor and I'm only a yellow belt.
jokes.setup: Britta is hitting Rick emotionally
jokes.punchline: Requests he crouch so she can hit him properly according to martial arts rules
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - applying martial arts etiquette to emotional violence. Perfect Britta mix of earnest and ridiculous.

jokes.text: The Fit combines the efficiency of a sub-compact with the versatility to take whatever life throws at you.
jokes.setup: Rick is having emotional reunion with Britta
jokes.punchline: Launches into Honda sales pitch while supposedly being authentic
jokes.explanation: Shows Rick can't turn off the marketing even in intimate moments. The irony of 'whatever life throws at you' is solid.

jokes.text: Shh! You're not allowed to know that. I'm useless to Honda, if anyone knows.
jokes.setup: Britta asks if he's guerrilla marketing for Honda
jokes.punchline: Confirms it while explaining why it should be secret
jokes.explanation: Good joke about the paradox of guerrilla marketing. Rick's earnest explanation of his own deception works.

jokes.text: As friend, Britta, can I at least show you the CR-V's easy fold-down 60-40 split row seat?
jokes.setup: Rick agrees to just be friends with Britta
jokes.punchline: Immediately tries to use friendship to show off car features
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rick - can't separate personal relationships from sales opportunities. The specific car feature makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Are you gonna play pretty please, will you like us more?
jokes.setup: Group is planning a game night to win over Elroy
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically names the obvious subtext of their game choice
jokes.explanation: Direct callout of the group's transparent attempt to manipulate Elroy. Good meta-awareness of social dynamics.

jokes.text: Better. In celebration of Elroy's life peaking in the early 90s, The Ears have it.
jokes.setup: Annie explains their game choice for Elroy
jokes.punchline: Casual brutal assessment that his life peaked decades ago
jokes.explanation: Annie's cheerful delivery of harsh assessment is perfect. The casual cruelty disguised as consideration is well-crafted.

jokes.text: What's the age allowance on The Ears Have It? Seven and up. Then I'm six.
jokes.setup: Discussion of being mature enough for the game
jokes.punchline: Jeff immediately opts out by claiming to be below minimum age
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff - using loopholes to avoid participation. The immediate response and logic are well-executed.

jokes.text: There's 35.2 cubic feet of cargo space back here.
jokes.setup: Rick and Britta are hiding in the back of his car
jokes.punchline: Rick turns intimate hiding into sales pitch with specific measurements
jokes.explanation: Rick's compulsive marketing even during romance. The specific measurement makes it funnier and more authentic.

jokes.text: Why choose, when you can Hond-oose.
jokes.setup: Dean appears with multiple Honda vehicles
jokes.punchline: Creates terrible pun combining Honda with 'choose'
jokes.explanation: Terrible pun that's funny because it's terrible and perfectly Dean. The forced wordplay is appropriately cringe.

jokes.text: My God, you're level seven susceptible.
jokes.setup: Dean shows his Honda obsession
jokes.punchline: Rick identifies Dean as highest level of marketing susceptibility
jokes.explanation: Establishes 'level seven susceptible' as specific term. Good worldbuilding and perfectly explains Dean's behavior.

jokes.text: I was thinking dolphin, but I knew they didn't have ears because my dentist has a poster of one on the ceiling, and I recently had a root canal.
jokes.setup: Shane explaining his thought process in the game
jokes.punchline: Connects dolphin anatomy to dental work through convoluted logic chain
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific logic chain. Shane's overly detailed explanation for simple deduction is funny.

jokes.text: We know you have your choice of friends and we thank you for choosing us.
jokes.setup: Elroy is leaving their game night
jokes.punchline: Annie uses airline departure announcement language for friendship
jokes.explanation: Clever application of corporate language to personal relationships. Annie's earnestness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I got up to make nachos and Elroy said, that was not-cho job. And we laughed. Then he made them for me. It was more than word play. It really wasn't my job in his eyes.
jokes.setup: Annie explains why Elroy's pun was meaningful
jokes.punchline: Realizes the pun revealed actual judgment about her role
jokes.explanation: Brilliant layered joke - pun that reveals character dynamics. Annie's realization adds depth to simple wordplay.

jokes.text: I'm cheating at a game of hide and seek right now. We weren't supposed to leave the rec center.
jokes.setup: Shane admits he cheats at everything
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's currently cheating at completely different game elsewhere
jokes.explanation: Good character reveal through specific example. The casual admission of multi-game cheating is funny.

jokes.text: Stupid sink! Every time! Mother. I hate.
jokes.setup: Bartender presumably hits sink while working
jokes.punchline: String of increasingly frustrated exclamations ending mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with escalating frustration. The cut-off curse adds to the effect.

jokes.text: This one's got a pig on the bottle.
jokes.setup: Bartender recommending scotch to customer
jokes.punchline: Offers completely irrelevant detail about bottle decoration as selling point
jokes.explanation: Good contrast with proper scotch description. The irrelevant pig detail is absurd in context.

jokes.text: You mean shill-boards and con-mercials?
jokes.setup: Honda representative asks about billboards and commercials
jokes.punchline: Britta creates portmanteau words revealing advertising's deceptive nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta activism through wordplay. The portmanteau words are clever and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: He finds that part of each life that Honda can improve and gently bathes it in the most helpful information possible.
jokes.setup: Honda rep defending guerrilla marketing
jokes.punchline: Uses absurdly gentle, spa-like language to describe manipulation
jokes.explanation: 'Gently bathes' is perfectly absurd corporate euphemism for psychological manipulation.

jokes.text: Did you trick me? Am I suffering? Do you love this man?
jokes.setup: Britta realizes she was influenced to buy expensive scotch
jokes.punchline: Questions escalate from marketing to existential to romantic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from practical to philosophical to personal. Shows Britta's thought process clearly.

jokes.text: Sorry, for being obnoxious!
jokes.setup: Todd makes sexual comment about CR-V affecting his relationship
jokes.punchline: Apologizes for being obnoxious while being more obnoxious
jokes.explanation: Classic Todd - fake apology that makes him more annoying. Character-appropriate behavior.

jokes.text: Man you got him pegged, man, pegged.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Rick being special to Britta
jokes.punchline: Todd's awkward repetition of 'pegged' with sexual undertones
jokes.explanation: Typical Todd innuendo. Not particularly clever but fits character's inappropriate tendencies.

jokes.text: Unless I need wrench made of licorice.
jokes.setup: Elroy tells Jeff to stay away from mechanic
jokes.punchline: Adds nonsensical exception about candy tool
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random Elroy absurdism. The specificity of 'licorice wrench' makes it funnier than generic randomness.

jokes.text: Man, I'm like a fart to that guy. He can't get away fast enough.
jokes.setup: Jeff realizes Elroy avoids him
jokes.punchline: Uses crude but effective analogy for being repulsive
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor from Jeff. The fart analogy is crude but effective and relatable.

jokes.text: Natalie is freezing. Natalie is freezing. Natalie is freezing.
jokes.setup: Jeff keeps mispronouncing band name 'Natalie's Freezing'
jokes.punchline: Gets stuck in loop of wrong pronunciation with increasing intensity
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff getting increasingly flustered. The repetition builds absurdity but isn't groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Because that's moon man talk.
jokes.setup: Annie asks why Dean wouldn't admit to being level seven susceptible
jokes.punchline: Dismisses the concept as crazy person nonsense with specific term
jokes.explanation: 'Moon man talk' is perfect Annie phrase - specific, dismissive, and slightly old-fashioned.

jokes.text: I can't ask any questions right now. Because all of the questions I have right now are rhetorical and they end with the word idiot.
jokes.setup: Annie says she has a rule about being constructive
jokes.punchline: Explains all her questions would be insults disguised as questions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Annie's struggle between niceness and frustration. The structure reveals her thought process perfectly.

jokes.text: Should we be caring more about our floor mats, George?
jokes.setup: Rick mentions quality floor mats in his Honda
jokes.punchline: Mom questions their own floor mat standards after hearing Rick's enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good example of guerrilla marketing working. The parents' confusion about floor mat importance is relatable.

jokes.text: No thank you, that movie is three hours of puke.
jokes.setup: Parents offer to watch Avatar movie
jokes.punchline: Britta gives characteristically harsh dismissal in crude terms
jokes.explanation: Pure Britta contrarianism. The crude dismissal of popular movie is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You weird, hair gelled, CPR dummy.
jokes.setup: Elroy is angry at Jeff for booking his ex's band
jokes.punchline: String of specific, cutting insults comparing Jeff to lifeless mannequin
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Elroy insult with specific, visual elements. 'CPR dummy' is particularly cutting for Jeff's vanity.

jokes.text: Why would anyone in the band be Natalie? We're artists.
jokes.setup: Jeff asks if band member is Natalie from 'Natalie is Freezing'
jokes.punchline: Julie dismisses obvious assumption with pretentious artist declaration
jokes.explanation: Good mockery of pretentious band logic. The 'we're artists' dismissal is perfectly pompous.

jokes.text: When I influence people to buy Honda products, I feel God's pleasure.
jokes.setup: Rick explains his devotion to Honda marketing
jokes.punchline: Uses religious language from Chariots of Fire for corporate sales
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Rick's corporate devotion to religious levels. The Chariots of Fire reference adds layers.

jokes.text: What's that thing that castles have? A moat. It goes over the moat.
jokes.setup: Elroy struggling to remember word while making metaphor
jokes.punchline: Can't remember 'drawbridge' despite describing its function perfectly
jokes.explanation: Relatable frustration of having word on tip of tongue. Sets up later payoff when he remembers.

jokes.text: One mechanical alligator?
jokes.setup: Elroy struggling to remember 'drawbridge'
jokes.punchline: Britta's guess combines his clues in absurd way
jokes.explanation: Good absurd synthesis of Elroy's clues. 'Mechanical alligator' is appropriately ridiculous guess.

jokes.text: A drawbridge, a drawbridge.
jokes.setup: Long struggle to remember the word throughout scene
jokes.punchline: Finally remembers 'drawbridge' with relief and repetition
jokes.explanation: Satisfying payoff to extended setup. The relief in finally remembering is relatable and well-executed.

jokes.text: Guess I'm a level seven susceptible too.
jokes.setup: Britta agrees to help Rick with one last Honda marketing job
jokes.punchline: Realizes she's also highly susceptible to marketing influence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Britta's self-awareness about her own weakness. Nice callback to established concept.

jokes.text: That's you. That's my impression of you, you jaded hipster a-hole.
jokes.setup: Julie says 'Fine' dismissively to Elroy
jokes.punchline: Explains it was impression of him, then insults him directly
jokes.explanation: Clever way to deliver insult through 'impression' device. Shows Julie's vindictive artist personality.

jokes.text: The 2015 Honda CR-V sells itself. That's why it's this years Motor Trend Sport Utility of the Year.
jokes.setup: Rick claims he had nothing to do with successful marketing
jokes.punchline: Immediately launches into detailed product pitch with awards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rick inability to stop marketing even when claiming innocence. The specific award detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Obviously you can't ask what you are. Mom, change your ears.
jokes.setup: Family playing 'Ears Have It' game incorrectly
jokes.punchline: Child corrects rules while giving impossible instruction about ear changing
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic of child applying game rules literally. 'Change your ears' is appropriately nonsensical.

jokes.text: How in the hell did you -. Am I an animal? Not by a long shot.
jokes.setup: Dad amazed someone guessed his elephant ears correctly
jokes.punchline: Mom's dismissive response that he's definitely not an animal
jokes.explanation: Good married couple dynamic with mom's matter-of-fact dismissal. 'Not by a long shot' adds emphasis.

jokes.text: I think we did something wrong. I know we did, Deb.
jokes.setup: Family has been playing The Ears Have It completely wrong
jokes.punchline: Understated admission of obvious failure
jokes.explanation: Gentle ending to the family game confusion. The understatement works as a quiet button to the episode.
